verses.latin_vulgate: Eripe me de inimicis meis, Deus meus,  et ab insurgentibus in me libera me.
verses.king_james: Deliver me from mine enemies, O my God: defend me from them that rise up against me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Eripe me de operantibus iniquitatem,  et de viris sanguinum salva me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭צִּילֵנִי מִפֹּ֣עֲלֵי אָ֑וֶן וּֽמֵאַנְשֵׁ֥י דָ֝מִ֗ים הוֹשִׁיעֵֽנִי׃
verses.king_james: Deliver me from the workers of iniquity, and save me from bloody men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia ecce ceperunt animam meam ;  irruerunt in me fortes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י הִנֵּ֪ה אָרְב֡וּ לְנַפְשִׁ֗י יָג֣וּרוּ עָלַ֣י עַזִּ֑ים לֹֽא־פִשְׁעִ֖י וְלֹא־חַטָּאתִ֣י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: For, lo, they lie in wait for my soul: the mighty are gathered against me; not for my transgression, nor for my sin, O Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque iniquitas mea, neque peccatum meum, Domine ;  sine iniquitate cucurri, et direxi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽלִי־עָ֭וֺן יְרֻצ֣וּן וְיִכּוֹנָ֑נוּ ע֖וּרָה לִקְרָאתִ֣י וּרְאֵֽה׃
verses.king_james: They run and prepare themselves without my fault: awake to help me, and behold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurge in occursum meum, et vide :  et tu, Domine Deus virtutum, Deus Israël,  intende ad visitandas omnes gentes :  non miserearis omnibus qui operantur iniquitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַתָּ֤ה יְהֹוָֽה־אֱלֹהִ֥ים ׀ צְבָא֡וֹת אֱלֹ֘הֵ֤י יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל הָקִ֗יצָה לִפְקֹ֥ד כׇּֽל־הַגּוֹיִ֑ם אַל־תָּחֹ֨ן כׇּל־בֹּ֖גְדֵי אָ֣וֶן סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Thou therefore, O Lord God of hosts, the God of Israel, awake to visit all the heathen: be not merciful to any wicked transgressors. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convertentur ad vesperam, et famem patientur ut canes :  et circuibunt civitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יָשׁ֣וּבוּ לָ֭עֶרֶב יֶהֱמ֥וּ כַכָּ֗לֶב וִיס֥וֹבְבוּ עִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: They return at evening: they make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce loquentur in ore suo,  et gladius in labiis eorum : quoniam quis audivit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֤ה ׀ יַבִּ֘יע֤וּן בְּפִיהֶ֗ם חֲ֭רָבוֹת בְּשִׂפְתֽוֹתֵיהֶ֑ם כִּי־מִ֥י שֹׁמֵֽעַ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, they belch out with their mouth: swords are in their lips: for who, say they, doth hear?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tu, Domine, deridebis eos ;  ad nihilum deduces omnes gentes.
verses.king_james: But thou, O Lord, shalt laugh at them; thou shalt have all the heathen in derision.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fortitudinem meam ad te custodiam,  quia, Deus, susceptor meus es :
verses.king_james: Because of his strength will I wait upon thee: for God is my defence.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus meus misericordia ejus præveniet me.
verses.king_james: The God of my mercy shall prevent me: God shall let me see my desire upon mine enemies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus ostendet mihi super inimicos meos :  ne occidas eos, nequando obliviscantur populi mei.  Disperge illos in virtute tua,  et depone eos, protector meus, Domine :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תַּהַרְגֵ֤ם ׀ פֶּֽן־יִשְׁכְּח֬וּ עַמִּ֗י הֲנִיעֵ֣מוֹ בְ֭חֵילְךָ וְהוֹרִידֵ֑מוֹ מָ֖גִנֵּ֣נוּ אֲדֹנָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Slay them not, lest my people forget: scatter them by thy power; and bring them down, O Lord our shield.

verses.latin_vulgate: delictum oris eorum, sermonem labiorum ipsorum ;  et comprehendantur in superbia sua.  Et de execratione et mendacio annuntiabuntur
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַטַּאת־פִּ֗ימוֹ דְּֽבַר־שְׂפָ֫תֵ֥ימוֹ וְיִלָּכְד֥וּ בִגְאוֹנָ֑ם וּמֵאָלָ֖ה וּמִכַּ֣חַשׁ יְסַפֵּֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: For the sin of their mouth and the words of their lips let them even be taken in their pride: and for cursing and lying which they speak.

verses.latin_vulgate: in consummatione :  in ira consummationis, et non erunt.  Et scient quia Deus dominabitur Jacob, et finium terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּלֵּ֥ה בְחֵמָה֮ כַּלֵּ֢ה וְֽאֵ֫ינֵ֥מוֹ וְֽיֵדְע֗וּ כִּֽי־אֱ֭לֹהִים מֹשֵׁ֣ל בְּֽיַעֲקֹ֑ב לְאַפְסֵ֖י הָאָ֣רֶץ סֶֽלָה׃
verses.king_james: Consume them in wrath, consume them, that they may not be: and let them know that God ruleth in Jacob unto the ends of the earth. Selah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Convertentur ad vesperam, et famem patientur ut canes :  et circuibunt civitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָשֻׁ֣בוּ לָ֭עֶרֶב יֶהֱמ֥וּ כַכָּ֗לֶב וִיס֥וֹבְבוּ עִֽיר׃
verses.king_james: And at evening let them return; and let them make a noise like a dog, and go round about the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipsi dispergentur ad manducandum ;  si vero non fuerint saturati, et murmurabunt.
verses.king_james: Let them wander up and down for meat, and grudge if they be not satisfied.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem cantabo fortitudinem tuam,  et exsultabo mane misericordiam tuam :  quia factus es susceptor meus,  et refugium meum in die tribulationis meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנִ֤י ׀ אָשִׁ֣יר עֻזֶּךָ֮ וַאֲרַנֵּ֥ן לַבֹּ֗קֶר חַ֫סְדֶּ֥ךָ כִּֽי־הָיִ֣יתָ מִשְׂגָּ֣ב לִ֑י וּ֝מָנ֗וֹס בְּי֣וֹם צַר־לִֽי׃
verses.king_james: But I will sing of thy power; yea, I will sing aloud of thy mercy in the morning: for thou hast been my defence and refuge in the day of my trouble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adjutor meus, tibi psallam,  quia Deus susceptor meus es ;  Deus meus, misericordia mea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֻ֭זִּי אֵלֶ֣יךָ אֲזַמֵּ֑רָה כִּֽי־אֱלֹהִ֥ים מִ֝שְׂגַּבִּ֗י אֱלֹהֵ֥י חַסְדִּֽי׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Unto thee, O my strength, will I sing: for God is my defence, and the God of my mercy.
